Solow Growth Model

Durabuild Case Study -- France, US, China Comparative Simulation

Y = A · Kα · L1-α
Country Parameters
Savings Rate (s) 20%
Depreciation (δ) 4.4%
Population Growth (n) 1.0%
Tech Growth (g) 1.5%
Capital Share (α) 0.30
Initial TFP (A0) 1.00
Initial Capital per Worker (k0) 10.0
Labor Participation (N/POP) 1.00
Case Study Presets
Steady State Values
--
k* (Capital/Worker)
--
y* (Output/Worker)
--
SS Growth Rate
--
Years to 90% SS
From the case: France's GDP/capita was 54% of the US in 1950 with a capital-to-labor ratio under 10% of the US. By 2000, France matched the US capital-to-labor ratio but GDP/capita was only 75% of the US -- the gap is explained by lower labor participation (N/POP = 0.72) and labor market rigidities.

GDP per Capita Transition Path

Output per effective worker converging to steady state over 50 years

Capital per Effective Worker

k̃ accumulation toward steady state k̃*

Solow Diagram

Investment vs. break-even line

Growth Rate of GDP per Capita Over Time

Transitional dynamics: growth declines as economy approaches steady state

Growth Decomposition

Sources of growth at year 1 vs year 40

Case Study: Exhibit 2 Data

France / United States ratios from the case
YearY/POPA(K/N)^0.3N/POP
19500.540.990.441.25
19800.861.050.880.93
20000.751.021.010.72
Y/POP = A · (K/N)^α · (N/POP)
France caught up in capital intensity but fell behind in labor participation. The TFP ratio stayed near 1, confirming similar technology access.

Comparative Scenario: France vs US vs China

GDP per capita index (Year 0 = 100) using case-calibrated parameters
